findings.evidence: `rg` hits: lines 71, 82, and 83 are `change` steps converting `φ : H` action goals/hypotheses to `(φ : Deck p) • e'`, exactly the undocumented wrapper/coercion defeq the proof-quality rubric asks to avoid.
findings.fix: Add a small reusable lemma, e.g. `subgroup_smul_eq_deck_smul (φ : H) (e : E) : φ • e = ((φ : Deck p) • e)`, and rewrite with it instead of relying on definally equal subgroup-action wrappers.
findings.issue: `eq_proj_of_subgroupOrbitRel` and `subgroupOrbitQuotientMap` use `change` to identify the `H`-action with the ambient `Deck p` action through subgroup coercions.

summary: The new proofs are mostly short, but the subgroup-action bridge relies on `change` across subgroup/ambient deck coercions. Factor that coercion into an explicit lemma so later Mathlib action/coercion changes do not silently break these proofs.
